message:
feat(runtime): add work-stealing parallel scheduler and expand monitor/HTTP/IO backend test coverage

Parallel runtime gains a work-stealing task queue implementation: idle workers
scan siblings' queues before parking, reducing tail latency when tasks are
unevenly distributed. asx_parallel_steal() picks the busiest peer and dequeues
from the tail (LIFO steal, FIFO local) with an atomic flag to prevent
contention. Adds asx_parallel_config_set_steal_enabled() to the config surface.

Monitor tests: Adds test_monitor_reports_healthy_after_init asserting that
the health monitor reports ASX_STATUS_OK after runtime initialization, covering
the newly wired monitor integration.

HTTP tests: Adds test_http_config_set_listen_addr_round_trips verifying
CONFIG SET/GET for HTTP listen address.

Runtime IO backend tests: Extends the IO backend test coverage with
test_runtime_io_backend_name_returns_ghost verifying the default backend
reports "ghost" when queried via asx_io_backend_str().

- src/runtime/parallel.c: work-stealing implementation
- tests/unit/monitor/test_monitor.c: health check test
- tests/unit/net/test_http.c: listen addr round-trip test
- tests/unit/runtime/test_rt.c: IO backend name test
- Makefile: add test_monitor target
- docs/PROFILE_RESOURCE_CLASS_CAPABILITY_MATRIX.md: update parallel row
- .beads/issues.jsonl: close bd-x3m4 (parallel steal)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1eqo.9.3","title":"Implement canonical examples, walkthroughs, and deterministic smoke-script packs","description":"Plan and implement the canonical user-facing example set that proves the port is usable, teachable, and diagnosable: quick-start workflows, scenario smoke packs, replayable demos, and operator-facing walkthroughs with deterministic artifacts. This is not documentation garnish; for many users, these examples are the product surface they touch first.","design":"Scope includes:
- curated example scenarios covering quick-start scope/task usage, channel and timeout flows, lab replay, doctor/report export, and native bootstrap/shutdown paths;
- example manifests/scripts that can be run deterministically in CI and locally;
- structured logs, summary reports, artifact pointers, and replay commands emitted by each example pack;
- lightweight validation helpers or unit tests for example manifests/templates so examples do not silently rot.

The examples should be organized as canonical acceptance slices rather than random demos. Each should teach one user workflow clearly while also doubling as a deterministic smoke test with rich diagnostics.","acceptance_criteria":"A. The roadmap has a dedicated bead for canonical examples and walkthroughs rather than assuming downstream implementation beads will incidentally produce them.
B. Every example pack includes a deterministic smoke script or e2e runner with detailed phase-by-phase logging, summary output, and artifact retention rules.
C. Comprehensive validation includes both unit-scale checks for example manifests/templates/helpers and full end-to-end runs for the example workflows themselves.
D. The example set covers both success and failure teaching cases so users can see what healthy and unhealthy runs look like.
E. Outputs are self-diagnosing enough that a future maintainer can understand a failed example run from its logs and generated artifacts alone.","notes":"Rust references motivating this bead include README quick examples, the documented example/cookbook surfaces, and deterministic CLI/lab workflows. This bead exists because “the runtime is theoretically powerful” is not the same as “users can successfully adopt and verify it.”","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T17:49:00.049349823Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T03:27:23.444662858Z","closed_at":"2026-03-13T03:27:23.440615844Z","close_reason":"Canonical examples and deterministic smoke packs complete. 5 example programs (ex_quickstart, ex_cancel_drain, ex_channel_flow, ex_timeout_deadline, ex_lab_replay) covering PACK-ONBOARD-CORE, PACK-STRUCTURED-RUNTIME, PACK-CHANNEL-SYNC, and PACK-LAB-REPLAY workflows. 16 scenarios across 5 examples, all passing. examples_smoke.sh e2e runner integrated into Makefile (26/26 pass). Full test suite green: 88 unit tests, 18 e2e scripts, all -Werror clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","core-parity","e2e","examples","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1eqo.9.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1eqo.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T17:49:00.049349823Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1eqo.9.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1eqo.11.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T17:49:00.049349823Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1eqo.9.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1eqo.14.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T17:49:00.049349823Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1eqo.9.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1eqo.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T17:49:00.049349823Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1eqo.9.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1eqo.2.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T17:49:00.049349823Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1eqo.9.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1eqo.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T17:49:00.049349823Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1eqo.9.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1eqo.9.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T17:49:00.049349823Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-1md","title":"EPIC: Conformance, Differential Fuzzing, and Test Completeness","description":"Background:
Port correctness is proven, not asserted. We need fixture parity, stochastic drift detection, and detailed diagnostics for fast triage.

Goal:
Build full test stack: unit, invariant, stress, e2e, conformance, and differential fuzzing with minimization.

Key requirement:
Tests and scripts must produce rich logs/artifacts so failures are actionable and reproducible.

Why this serves users:
- high confidence in semantic faithfulness
- lower MTTR for regressions
- safer optimization velocity.

Cross-cutting quality contract:
- Child tasks must define and link unit tests, e2e script coverage (when behavioral), and structured log/manifest artifacts or explicit rationale for non-applicability.
- Completion evidence must remain compatible with bd-66l.9 enforcement and traceability index expectations.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:47:42.246521936Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T23:12:19.923521668Z","closed_at":"2026-02-27T23:12:19.923498053Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","fuzz","quality","testing"]}
 {"id":"bd-1md.1","title":"Build Rust reference fixture capture tooling for kernel semantics","description":"Background:
Conformance depends on a trusted Rust behavior oracle encoded as canonical fixtures. This bead is the tooling foundation used by fixture-capture streams.

Subtasks:
- Implement fixture capture program skeleton, canonical schema writer, and provenance metadata emitter.
- Implement deterministic capture execution pipeline with stable scenario IDs and seeds.
- Provide capture CLI interfaces used by core/robustness/vertical fixture-family tasks.
- Document tooling usage and output contracts for downstream conformance/e2e stages.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Tooling can capture and emit schema-valid canonical fixture artifacts reproducibly.
- Outputs include provenance and version metadata.
- Downstream fixture-family tasks (bd-1md.13..bd-1md.15) can execute against this tooling contract.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:50:14.636055695Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T04:30:43.767743939Z","closed_at":"2026-02-27T04:30:43.767722058Z","close_reason":"Published deterministic fixture capture tooling contract (CLI + pipeline + provenance), added canonical manifest/fixture schemas, and wired traceability links for downstream fixture-family beads","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","fixtures","rust-ref"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1md.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1md","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1md.1","depends_on_id":"bd-296.12","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1md.1","depends_on_id":"bd-296.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1md.1","depends_on_id":"bd-296.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1md.10","title":"Implement canonical e2e scenario script suite with deterministic run-manifest artifacts","description":"Background:
The plan requires comprehensive e2e coverage with rich diagnostics; this bead is an aggregation checkpoint over dedicated e2e script families.

Subtasks:
- Integrate outputs from e2e family beads bd-1md.16 through bd-1md.18.
- Standardize script inputs (seed, scenario pack, profile, codec, resource class) and output locations.
- Emit run manifest per e2e invocation containing run_id, git rev, compiler/target, seed, scenario IDs, profile/codec matrix, semantic digests, and first-divergence pointers.
- Add triage-friendly first-failure summary output and rerun command snippets.

Acceptance Criteria:
- E2E scripts are one-command reproducible locally and in CI.
- Every failing e2e run writes complete logs and artifact manifests.
- Scripts are mapped to hard gates and release verification workflows.
- Completion requires all e2e family streams (bd-1md.16..bd-1md.18) to be merged and validated.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:01:44.012809460Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T22:13:41.875147815Z","closed_at":"2026-02-27T22:13:41.875128739Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","logging","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1md.10","depends_on_id":"bd-1md","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1md.10","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1md.10","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1md.10","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.16","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1md.10","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.17","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1md.10","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.18","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1md.10","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1md.10","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.20","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-3vt.8","title":"Prototype time-virtualization layer for jitter and clock-anomaly replay","description":"Background:
Idea round 3 highlights time virtualization as a lever for reproducing jitter and clock anomalies deterministically.

Subtasks:
- Define virtual-time injection API boundaries and scheduling interactions.
- Extend scenario DSL with clock anomaly events (jitter, stall, jump).
- Build replay tests showing deterministic reproduction of anomaly scenarios.
- Evaluate operational overhead and integration risks.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Provides clear go/no-go recommendation with benchmarked overhead.
- Anomaly scenarios reproduce deterministically across repeated runs.
- Fallback to physical-time mode remains intact and documented.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:57:19.503981512Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-01T07:09:49.925389641Z","closed_at":"2026-03-01T07:09:49.925365135Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["advanced","determinism","replay"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3vt.8","depends_on_id":"bd-2n0.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3vt.8","depends_on_id":"bd-3vt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3vt.8","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.13","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3vt.9","title":"Define hot-reload-safe config boundaries and semantic freeze points","description":"Background:
Idea round 3 proposes hot-reload-safe config boundaries so runtime tuning does not mutate semantics mid-flight.

Subtasks:
- Classify config fields as hot-reload-safe, freeze-at-start, or restart-required.
- Define semantic freeze points where updates are applied atomically.
- Add tests for mid-run update safety and rollback on invalid updates.
- Document operator workflow for safe live tuning.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Config reload behavior is deterministic and policy-driven.
- Unsafe mid-run updates are rejected with explicit diagnostics.
- Replay and semantic digest guarantees remain intact.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:57:19.586821141Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-01T20:14:59.210085015Z","closed_at":"2026-03-01T20:14:59.210057855Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["advanced"]}

 {"id":"bd-56t","title":"EPIC: Release, Evidence, and Operational Readiness","description":"Background:
A high-assurance runtime needs repeatable release mechanics plus evidence bundles for audits and regulated contexts.

Goal:
Define release automation, artifact integrity checks, and evidence packaging that map implementation behavior to documented guarantees.

Outputs:
- release runbooks
- signed/checksummed artifacts
- certification-ready evidence skeletons

Why this serves users:
- production confidence for operators
- reduced friction for safety-critical adoption.

Cross-cutting quality contract:
- Child tasks must define and link unit tests, e2e script coverage (when behavioral), and structured log/manifest artifacts or explicit rationale for non-applicability.
- Completion evidence must remain compatible with bd-66l.9 enforcement and traceability index expectations.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:47:42.559479025Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-01T06:06:02.706966537Z","closed_at":"2026-03-01T06:06:02.706944196Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["evidence","ops","release"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-56t","depends_on_id":"bd-66l","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-56t.1","title":"Implement release automation and integrity artifacts for asx targets","description":"Background:
Releases must be repeatable and verifiable across supported targets.

Subtasks:
- Define build and packaging pipeline for target artifacts.
- Emit checksum and signature bundles for each release asset.
- Define asset naming/versioning conventions and provenance metadata.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Release pipeline is reproducible and documented.
- Integrity metadata is generated automatically.
- Release artifacts map cleanly to CI evidence and version tags.","notes":"Implemented in this session: tools/ci/run_release_artifacts.sh (deterministic tar.xz + sha256 + sigstore bundle + provenance metadata for binary/source), .github/workflows/release.yml (tag-triggered v* automation + validation + publish), Makefile release-artifacts target, docs/QUALITY_GATES.md gate entry, README release artifact contract. Local validation: bash -n script OK; source and linux-x86_64 artifact generation OK. rch suite: format-check/lint/build pass; test/conformance hit remote permission issues in worker context, unrelated to this release-flow code path.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"FoggyCliff","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:51:14.113965360Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-01T05:13:34.482371111Z","closed_at":"2026-03-01T05:13:34.482350553Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["artifacts","automation","release"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-56t.1","depends_on_id":"bd-56t","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-56t.1","depends_on_id":"bd-66l.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-56t.2","title":"Create release verification and rollback runbook","description":"Background:
Operational users need deterministic post-release validation and safe rollback procedures.

Subtasks:
- Define verification checklist for artifact presence, signatures, and smoke tests.
- Require release-candidate e2e runs (kernel lifecycle, codec equivalence, profile parity, restart continuity, vertical HFT/automotive/router lanes) with archived manifests.
- Define rollback triggers and rollback procedure by release class.
- Define incident-response references for replay/conformance validation post-deploy.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Runbook enables repeatable release validation by any maintainer.
- Rollback path is fast and tested in at least one dry run.
- Includes profile-specific smoke verification guidance.
- Release approval requires complete structured logs and artifact manifests from required e2e runs.","notes":"Implemented release verification/rollback runbook in docs/DEPLOYMENT_HARDENING.md with: release classes, deterministic pre-publish checklist, required artifact/log manifest set, profile-specific smoke requirements, rollback triggers, rollback procedures by class, and post-deploy replay/conformance references. Added README link to runbook. Performed dry-run validation on 2026-03-01 using tools/ci/run_release_artifacts.sh for source+binary targets and checksum verification in build/release/dist.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"FoggyCliff","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:51:14.218832958Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-01T05:18:26.673220203Z","closed_at":"2026-03-01T05:18:26.673202419Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ops","release","runbook"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-56t.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-56t.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.18","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-56t.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-56t.2","depends_on_id":"bd-56t","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-56t.2","depends_on_id":"bd-56t.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-56t.2","depends_on_id":"bd-j4m.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-56t.2","depends_on_id":"bd-j4m.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-ekb3","title":"EPIC: Hardcoded Constants, Stale Comments, and Code Quality Remediation","description":"Master epic for systematically addressing all hardcoded magic numbers that should be configurable,
stale/misleading comments indicating unfinished code, and related code quality issues discovered
during a comprehensive fresh-eyes audit of the entire asupersync ANSI C codebase.

## Background

A full-project audit was conducted scanning every .c and .h file for:
1. Hardcoded numeric constants in function bodies that should be named #define macros
2. Comments containing "will", "would", "TODO", "FIXME" indicating unfinished or deferred work
3. Stale documentation that no longer matches the actual implementation state

## Scope

This epic covers:
- Converting 4 hardcoded magic numbers to named, #ifndef-guarded compile-time constants
- Updating 2 stale header comments that incorrectly describe code as stubs/future work
- Identifying and documenting items verified as correct (not needing changes)
- Ensuring all changes compile cleanly with -Werror and pass existing test suites

## Architectural Principle

The asx project uses a "resource contracts instead of silent degradation" philosophy (README).
Every operational parameter should be:
1. Named (self-documenting in code)
2. Overridable at compile time (#ifndef guard)
3. Documented with its default value and unit
4. Consistent with the profile/resource-class system

This ensures embedded deployers (R1/R2/R3 resource classes) and specialized profiles
(HFT, AUTOMOTIVE) can tune thresholds without modifying source code.

## Verification

All changes MUST:
- Build cleanly with `make build` (strict -Werror)
- Pass all existing unit tests
- Not change any runtime behavior when using default values
- Be backward-compatible (existing code that doesn't override the macros gets identical behavior)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:02:45.202825037Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:16:14.362635656Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:16:14.362076177Z","close_reason":"All tasks completed: 4 constants extracted, 2 comments fixed, audit trail documented","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","code-quality","constants"]}
 {"id":"bd-ekb3.1","title":"SUB-EPIC: Hardcoded Magic Numbers → Named Compile-Time Constants","description":"Convert all hardcoded magic numbers in function bodies to named #define macros with
#ifndef guards, enabling compile-time override by deployers.

## Why This Matters

The asx project targets 9 deployment profiles (CORE through BROWSER) and 3 resource
classes (R1/R2/R3). Each profile has different operational envelopes. Hardcoded constants
prevent deployers from tuning behavior without source modification, violating the
"resource contracts instead of silent degradation" principle.

## Pattern

For each constant:
1. Add `#ifndef ASX_<MODULE>_<NAME>` / `#define` / `#endif` in the relevant public header
2. Replace the literal in the .c file with the macro name
3. Update any doc comments referencing the old literal value
4. Verify the default value matches the original literal exactly (zero behavior change)

## Constants Identified

| Constant | File | Value | Unit | New Macro |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Process wait poll guard | process.c:235 | 10000 | polls | ASX_PROCESS_WAIT_MAX_POLLS |
| App default poll budget | app.c:125 | 10000 | polls | ASX_APP_DEFAULT_POLL_BUDGET |
| HFT p99 gate threshold | hft_instrument.c:281 | 10000 | nanoseconds | ASX_HFT_DEFAULT_P99_NS |
| Automotive watchdog period | automotive_instrument.c:250,258 | 10000000 | nanoseconds | ASX_AUTO_DEFAULT_WATCHDOG_PERIOD_NS |

## Items Audited and Verified as Correct (No Change Needed)

These were examined and confirmed to be intentionally placed:
- hooks.c:62 `return 0` — Intentional platform hook stub; overridden at runtime init
- lab.c:57,93 `1000000ULL` tick_ns — Default in config struct; already user-configurable via asx_lab_config
- console.c:84 "would check isatty()" — Design note explaining platform-specific behavior
- plan.c:413 "this would set up a deadline" — Explaining deterministic simulation vs real async
- Profile overload thresholds (90/85/80/75%) in overload_catalog.c — Intentional per-profile policy
- Seqlock spike retry limits (100, 1000) — Experimental research code, not production path","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:03:01.938649835Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:16:12.135003483Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:16:12.134303087Z","close_reason":"All 4 hardcoded constants extracted to named #ifndef-guarded macros","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["code-quality","constants","hardcoded"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ekb3.1","depends_on_id":"bd-ekb3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:03:01.938649835Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-ekb3.1.1","title":"Extract ASX_PROCESS_WAIT_MAX_POLLS from process.c:235","description":"## What

Replace the hardcoded `10000u` poll guard in asx_process_wait_with_output() with a named
compile-time constant ASX_PROCESS_WAIT_MAX_POLLS.

## Where

- Header: include/asx/process/process.h (add #ifndef/#define/#endif block)
- Source: src/process/process.c line 235 (replace literal with macro)

## Why

asx_process_wait_with_output() polls in a bounded loop to prevent infinite hangs when
waiting for a child process to exit. The 10,000 iteration limit was chosen as a reasonable
default, but embedded deployers on slow hardware may need more iterations, while
latency-sensitive HFT profiles may want fewer.

Without a named constant, the only way to change this is to edit source code directly,
which violates the profile/resource-class customization model.

## Implementation

```c
// In process.h, after ASX_PROCESS_PROGRAM_MAX:
#ifndef ASX_PROCESS_WAIT_MAX_POLLS
#define ASX_PROCESS_WAIT_MAX_POLLS 10000u
#endif

// In process.c:235:
uint32_t max_polls = ASX_PROCESS_WAIT_MAX_POLLS;
```

## Verification

- `make build` passes with -Werror
- All process-related tests pass
- Default behavior unchanged (10000 iterations when macro not overridden)

## Status: COMPLETED

This task has been implemented and verified.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:03:30.083578209Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:03:30.083578209Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:03:30.083578209Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["completed","constants","process"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ekb3.1.1","depends_on_id":"bd-ekb3.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:03:30.083578209Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 ### 8.1 Missing Fail-Closed Checks Before Parity Claims
